title: Lastrea rivulorum
intro: Lastrea rivulorum, also known as the Marsh Fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The Marsh Fern has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help in alleviating conditions like arthritis and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Marsh Fern is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, particularly those related to inflammation and oxidative stress.
taxonomy:
Lastrea rivulorum is classified in the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and family Athyriaceae. 
It belongs to the genus Lastrea, which is a group of ferns. 
The family Athyriaceae comprises about 120 species of ferns. 
This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Lastrea rivulorum. 
Further research and study may reveal more about its specific classification within the plant kingdom.
